description: Delivery failure / bounce notice (T9 / BOUNCE_OR_DELIVERY_NOTICE). Sent from a mailer-daemon address with DSN evidence, matched deterministically by det-bounce-001. No human review required, no reply, but flagged for data cleanup (suppress/remove the bouncing address from future sends).

input.reply_text: This is an automatically generated Delivery Status Notification. Delivery to the following recipient failed permanently: alex.diaz@prospectco.test. The error was: 550 5.1.1 The email account that you tried to reach does not exist.
input.reply_text_snippet: Delivery to the following recipient failed permanently: alex.diaz@prospectco.test.

expected.notes: from_address_email contains 'mailer-daemon' AND the reply text contains 'Delivery Status Notification' (dsnEvidence), so det-bounce-001 fires (not the weaker det-bounce-002). event_type=email_bounced is in SUPPORTED_EVENT_TYPES. 'automatically generated' does not match the AUTOMATED_RESPONSE_PATTERN ('automatic reply' / 'automated response' / 'auto-reply' / 'do not reply'), so is_automated_response=false here - this is expected and does not affect the bounce classification. data_cleanup_required=true is a flag only; no Instantly suppression call is made (external_action_status stays NOT_PERFORMED).
